description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has to double-check that a
troublesome application always writes its timestamps in a fully
predictable, locale-independent way.  
Create a single, **deterministic** log file that proves the shell is
using the correct locale (C) and time-zone (UTC).

Desired end-state of the system
1. A directory called /home/user/logs must exist.  
2. Inside that directory there must be a file called
   /home/user/logs/test_timestamp.log  
3. The file must contain **exactly one line, no leading or trailing
   spaces, no newline at the start or extra blank lines at the end.**
   The line must read:

   FixedUTC: 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z

   • “FixedUTC:” is literal and case-sensitive.  
   • The timestamp part must be the ISO-8601 representation of Unix
     epoch 0 in UTC.
4. The file must be world-readable (mode 644) so that the CI pipeline
   can pick it up.
5. The agent must not require sudo; rely on per-user environment
   variables only.

Hints
• You can satisfy items 1–4 with only 2–3 shell commands:
  – set both environment variables LANG=C and TZ=UTC
  – make the directory
  – generate the deterministic timestamp for epoch 0 and redirect it
    into the log file in the exact format shown above.

The automated checker will:

a) verify /home/user/logs exists and is a directory  
b) verify /home/user/logs/test_timestamp.log exists and is mode 644  
c) read the single line of that file and compare it literally to the
   required text

Produce no extra files or output; only the log file described above
will be inspected.
truth:
Expected artefact for verification
----------------------------------
Path           : /home/user/logs/test_timestamp.log
File mode      : 100644 (-rw-r--r--)
Exact contents : FixedUTC: 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z

No extra lines, spaces or trailing newline after the Z.

The directory /home/user/logs must exist prior to the file check and
may have mode 755 or less permissive.

The test suite will `stat` the file to confirm permissions and use
`cat -A` to detect hidden whitespace.

Environment variables LANG and TZ are not directly tested; their
correctness is implicitly validated because the timestamp for epoch 0
must match the fixed ISO-8601 value when produced by the `date`
utility under those settings.
